metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes (run_shell_command is_background); daemon_capability: bug (geminicli parser has NO background-process tracking — settles to ready at end_turn while the detached process is still alive); driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug; driver_capability: ready

Route: record-known-failing. The agent genuinely runs background processes; the geminicli adapter does not observe them today, so a recording will fail the spec until the parser is taught the Gemini background-spawn shape. We record it as known_failing (spec asserts the CORRECT arc) and file an issue — the trace exists, so this is product work in the adapter, not an architectural incapability.

## agent_supports: yes

Gemini CLI v0.46.0's `run_shell_command` / ShellTool ships a structured `is_background` parameter (binary strings: `[SHELL_PARAM_IS_BACKGROUND]`, `description: "Set to true if this command should be run in the background (e.g. for long-running servers or watchers). The command will be started, allowed to run for a brief moment to check for immediate errors, and then moved to the background."`). A backgrounded launch returns immediately with `returnDisplay: "Background process started with PID ${pid}."` and the tool result surfaces `Background PIDs: …` and `Process Group PGID: …` (the command is wrapped via `wrapCommandForBackgroundPIDs` writing PIDs to a temp file; setsid/disown/nohup process-group handling is present). There is also a separate "List Background Processes" tool (`description: "The process ID (PID) of the background process to inspect."`) — the polling analog to Claude's BashOutput. So the agent performs the full scenario behavior: spawn-and-return, then poll-to-exit.

## daemon_capability: bug (NOT incapable, NOT full)

The geminicli adapter (core/adapters/inbound/agents/geminicli/parser.go) has ZERO background-process tracking — grep for background/run_in_background/async across the package returns nothing. `parseAssistant` settles the session to `turn_done` (→ ready) the instant a `gemini` assistant message carries final text and opens no further tool calls (parser.go:209). The entire BackgroundProcessCount + HasLiveBackgroundProcess working-hold machinery (core/application/services/background_probe.go, core/domain/session) is wired EXCLUSIVELY from the claudecode parser's structured `toolUseResult.backgroundTaskId` field and the `tasks/<id>.output` lsof probe — both Claude-Code-specific shapes the probe docstring names explicitly ("the daemon's authoritative liveness check for Claude Code Bash background processes").

Gemini reports its background spawn DIFFERENTLY: as prose ("Background process started with PID N") inside the assistant message and `Background PIDs:` in the tool result text, writing stdout/stderr to a temp PID file — not a `backgroundTaskId` field and not a `tasks/<id>.output` path. The daemon therefore (1) never increments a count, (2) never gates IsAgentDone, and (3) settles the session to ready at the turn's end while the detached process is still alive — exactly the regression the scenario's "WHERE to look if it breaks" describes. This is `bug` not `incapable` because the trace DOES exist in the transcript the daemon already tails (the run_shell_command tool call with is_background + the Background-PID message); the parser merely ignores it. The fix is the Gemini parallel of #450/#445: recognize the Gemini background-spawn shape, surface BackgroundProcessCount, and probe the reported PID's liveness.

## driver_capability: ready

The recipe needs only send / wait_turn / sleep, all in the gemini-cli driver's DRIVE_ELICITS ("send slash wait_turn sleep"). No driver gap. wait_turn classifies turn-done the same way the parser does (a bare `gemini` message with non-empty text and no tool calls), so it brackets the single prompt that spawns-and-polls.

## Confidence 0.8

The agent surface is confirmed from the live v0.46.0 bundle strings (high). The daemon verdict is confirmed by reading the adapter source (parser has no background tracking; the working-hold is Claude-only). The 0.8 (not higher) reflects that the EXACT on-disk transcript shape Gemini writes for a backgrounded run — whether the is_background tool call and the Background-PID line survive into the JSONL the daemon tails, and how the model sequences the list-background poll — is only confirmed live by `record`. The verdict that the daemon mis-handles it holds regardless of that detail (there is no code path that could observe it), so record will confirm known_failing, not flip the pillar.
caveats: agent_supports=yes is confirmed against the live gemini v0.46.0 bundle: [SHELL_PARAM_IS_BACKGROUND] / is_background parameter on run_shell_command, 'Background process started with PID' result, 'Background PIDs:' + 'Process Group PGID:' in the tool result, and a 'List Background Processes' inspection tool.

daemon_capability=bug: the geminicli parser has no background-process tracking; BackgroundProcessCount + the working-hold are wired only from the claudecode backgroundTaskId/tasks-output shape. A recording will show the session flip working→ready at the turn's end (when the assistant message finalizes), NOT held working while the detached process is alive — so it fails bg_alive_working and the dwell assertion. File an issue and record known_failing.

Recording variance / provisional emission: the precise JSONL shape Gemini writes for an is_background run (does the spawn tool call + Background-PID line persist; how the model sequences the list-background poll) is confirmed only by the live recording. The bug verdict does not depend on it — no adapter code path observes a Gemini background process under any shape.
Auth/deadline: recording needs Google OAuth set up out-of-band; Gemini CLI stops serving Google One / unpaid tiers on 2026-06-18, after which recordings on those tiers fail auth.

sources.note: Confirms agent_supports=yes on the live gemini v0.46.0 bundle: run_shell_command/ShellTool ships is_background ('Set to true if this command should be run in the background … then moved to the background.'), returns 'Background process started with PID ${pid}.', surfaces 'Background PIDs:' + 'Process Group PGID:', and provides a 'List Background Processes' PID-inspection tool. is_background appears 168x (camelCase) + 30x (snake) across chunks.

daemon_capability=bug evidence: NO background-process tracking in the geminicli parser (grep background|run_in_background|async = NONE). parseAssistant (line ~209) settles to turn_done/ready as soon as a `gemini` message has final text and no open tool calls — so the session would flip to ready at the turn's end while a Gemini is_background process is still alive.

The working-hold liveness probe is documented as 'the daemon's authoritative liveness check for Claude Code Bash background processes (run_in_background)', driven by tasks/<bash_id>.output paths — a Claude-specific shape Gemini does not produce. Confirms no Gemini code path increments BackgroundProcessCount or gates IsAgentDone.

Reference for what WOULD be needed: BackgroundProcessCount/HasLiveBackgroundProcess are populated only from the structured toolUseResult.backgroundTaskId (line ~538) plus BashOutput/KillShell/<task-notification> closes — none of which exist in Gemini's transcript. The Gemini fix is the parallel of #450/#445 in the geminicli adapter.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH and authenticated via Google OAuth (v0.46.x ships the run_shell_command is_background parameter and the List Background Processes tool)

irrlichd is recording (isolated dev daemon with --record). NOTE: this cell is known_failing — the current geminicli adapter cannot observe a Gemini background process, so the recording is expected to fail the bg_alive_working / dwell phases until the parser learns the Gemini background-spawn shape (the #450/#445 parallel).
Gemini drives under `gemini -y` (yolo) so the run_shell_command tool auto-approves; the driver auto-accepts the first-run trust-folder prompt
setup: Driver launches gemini in a fresh trusted cwd under <staging>/cwd; no seed files — the background process is a self-contained sleep && echo.

script.text: Do exactly this and nothing else, in a single turn. Use the run_shell_command tool to run the command `sleep 40 && echo BG_DONE`, and set its is_background parameter to true so it launches in the background and the tool returns immediately with a background PID. As soon as that tool returns the background PID, reply with exactly the word: done. Do NOT poll, do NOT check whether it finished, do NOT run any other commands — the background process must still be running when you reply.

verify: transcript.jsonl contains a run_shell_command tool call whose input carries is_background:true, and a result reporting a Background PID (the spawn-and-return)
transcript.jsonl contains one or more list-background-processes tool calls (the poll-to-exit)

KNOWN FAILING: events.jsonl is EXPECTED to show the session settle working→ready at the turn's normal end (when the final `gemini` assistant message finalizes), NOT held working while the detached process is alive — so bg_alive_working and the dwell assertion fail. This is the geminicli adapter gap; clear known_failing once the parser surfaces BackgroundProcessCount + a liveness gate for Gemini background processes.
primary session is born ready and binds a PID within ~2s of launch
